name: Kubernetes Python Client

auth.notes: kubeconfig file, in-cluster service account, or explicit bearer token

performance.notes: API server round-trip; watch/stream for event-driven patterns

agent_readiness.notes: Official Python client for Kubernetes API. from kubernetes import client, config. config.load_kube_config() or config.load_incluster_config(). v1 = client.CoreV1Api(). pods = v1.list_namespaced_pod('default'). apps_v1 = client.AppsV1Api(). deploy = apps_v1.read_namespaced_deployment('name', 'ns'). Watch for events: watch.Watch().stream(v1.list_namespaced_pod). Custom resources via CustomObjectsApi. Auto-generated from Kubernetes OpenAPI spec. Async: kubernetes_asyncio package.

gotchas: Auto-generated API is verbose — kopf or kr8s provide better ergonomics
Watch streams require careful timeout and reconnect handling
Different API groups for different resource types (apps/v1, batch/v1, etc.)
Type names don't match kubectl commands exactly
best_when: Programmatic Kubernetes resource management from Python
Operators and controllers (use with kopf for better ergonomics)
Monitoring and alerting on K8s cluster state
avoid_when: When kr8s or kopf provide sufficient abstraction
Interactive K8s management (kubectl UI or Lens better)
summary: Official Python Kubernetes client — config.load_kube_config(); v1.list_namespaced_pod('default'). Auto-generated from OpenAPI spec. CoreV1Api, AppsV1Api, CustomObjectsApi. Watch streams for event-driven patterns. In-cluster and kubeconfig auth. Verbose API — consider kr8s or kopf for better ergonomics. Async support via kubernetes_asyncio package.
